sections.label: Section 4.5.718
sections.translation: You ’ ve hit upon the very thing .

sections.label: Section 4.5.718b
sections.translation: Lead me to him then .

sections.label: Section 4.5.718c
sections.translation: I won ’ t delay .

sections.label: Section 4.5.718d
sections.translation: When you ’ ve done so , go over to my wife , that she may call upon her before she goes away .

sections.label: Section 4.5.720
sections.original: Dicat nos dare eam Phormioni nuptum , ne succenseat ;
sections.translation: She must tell her that we are going to give her in marriage to Phormio , that she may not be angry with us ; and that he is a fitter match for her , as knowing more of her ; that we have in no way departed from our duty ; that as much has been given for a portion as he asked for .

sections.label: Section 4.5.723b
sections.translation: What the plague does that matter to you ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.723c
sections.translation: A great deal , Demipho .

sections.label: Section 4.5.724
sections.original: Non satis est tuum te officium facere , si non id fama approbat .
sections.translation: It is not enough for you to do your duty , if common report does not approve of it ;

sections.label: Section 4.5.725
sections.original: Volo ipsius quoque voluntate hoc fieri , ne se eiectam praedicet .
sections.translation: I wish all this to be done with her own sanction as well , that she mayn ’ t be saying that she has been turned out of doors .

sections.label: Section 4.5.726
sections.translation: I can do all that myself .

sections.label: Section 4.5.726b
sections.translation: It will come better from one woman to another .

sections.label: Section 4.5.727
sections.translation: I ’ ll ask her .

sections.label: Section 4.5.727b
sections.original: Ubi illas ego nunc reperire possim cogito .
sections.translation: I ’ m thinking where I can find them now . [*]

sections.label: Section 5.1.728
sections.original: Quid agam ? quem mihi amicum inveniam misera ? aut quo consilia haec referam ? aut
sections.translation: What am I to do ? What friend , in my distress , shall I find , to whom to disclose these plans ; and where shall I look for relief ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.730
sections.original: Nam vereor hera ne ob meum suasum indigne iniuria afficiatur :
sections.translation: For I ’ m afraid that my mistress , in consequence of my advice , may undeservingly sustain some injury , so extremely ill do I hear that the young man ’ s father takes what has happened .

sections.label: Section 5.1.732
sections.original: Nam quae haec anus est exanimata , a fratre quae egressa est meo ?
sections.translation: But what old woman ’ s this , that has come out of my brother ’ s house , half dead with fright ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.733
sections.original: Quod ut facerem egestas me impulit ; quum scirem infirmas nuptias
sections.translation: It was distress that compelled me to this step , though I knew that the match was not likely to hold good ; my object was , that in the mean time life might be supported .

sections.label: Section 5.1.735
sections.original: Certe edepol , nisi me animus fallit , aut parum prospiciunt oculi ,
sections.translation: Upon my faith , surely , unless my recollection deceives me , or my sight ’ s not very good , I espy my daughter ’ s nurse . [*]

sections.label: Section 5.1.736b
sections.translation: And we are not able to find —

sections.label: Section 5.1.736c
sections.translation: What must I do ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.737
sections.translation: Her father .

sections.label: Section 5.1.737b
sections.original: Adeo ? an maneo , dum ea quake loquitur magis cognosco ?
sections.translation: Shall I accost her , or shall I wait to learn more distinctly what it is she ’ s saying ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.738
sections.original: Quod si eum nunc reperire possim , nihil est quod verear .
sections.translation: If now I could find him , there ’ s nothing that I should be in fear of .

sections.label: Section 5.1.738b
sections.translation: ’ Tis the very woman . I ’ ll address her .

sections.label: Section 5.1.739b
sections.translation: Who ’ s that speaking here ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.739c
sections.translation: Sophrona .

sections.label: Section 5.1.739d
sections.translation: Mentioning my name , too ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.740
sections.translation: Look round at me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.740b
sections.original: Di , obsecro vos , estne hic Stilpho ?
sections.translation: Ye Gods , I do beseech you , isn ’ t this Stilpho ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.740c
sections.translation: No .

sections.label: Section 5.1.740d
sections.translation: Do you deny it ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.741
sections.original: Concede hinc a foribus paululum istorsum , sodes , Sophrona .
sections.translation: Step a little this way from that door , Sophrona , if you please ( pointing ) . Don ’ t you , henceforth , be calling me by that name .

sections.label: Section 5.1.742b
sections.translation: Why ? Pray , are you not the person you always used to say you were ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.743b
sections.translation: Hush ! ( pointing to his own house .)

sections.label: Section 5.1.743c
sections.translation: Why are you afraid about that door ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.744
sections.original: Conclusam hic habeo uxorem saevam : verum istoc de nomine
sections.translation: I have got a shrew of a wife shut up there . For by that name I formerly

sections.label: Section 5.1.745
sections.original: Eo perperam olim dixi ne vos forte imprudentes foris
sections.translation: falsely called myself , in order that you might not chance indiscreetly to blab it out of doors , and then my wife , by some means or other , might come to know of it .

sections.label: Section 5.1.747
sections.original: Istoc pol nos te hic invenire miserae nunquam potuimus .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , that ’ s the very reason why we , wretched creatures , have never been able to find you out here .

sections.label: Section 5.1.748
sections.original: Eho , die mihi , quid rei tibi est cum familia hac unde exis ?
sections.translation: Well , but tell me , what business have you with that family from whose house you were coming out ? Where are the ladies ? [*]

sections.label: Section 5.1.749b
sections.translation: Ah , wretched me !

sections.label: Section 5.1.749c
sections.translation: Hah ! What ’ s the matter ? Are they still alive ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.749d
sections.translation: Your daughter is alive .

sections.label: Section 5.1.750
sections.original: Matrem ipsam ex aegritudine miseram mors consecuta est .
sections.translation: Her poor mother died of grief .

sections.label: Section 5.1.751
sections.translation: An unfortunate thing !

sections.label: Section 5.1.751b
sections.original: Ego autem quae essem anus deserta , egens , ignota ,
sections.translation: As for me , being a lone old woman , in want , and unknown , I contrived , as well as I could , to get the young woman married to the young man who is master of this house ( pointing ) .

sections.label: Section 5.1.753b
sections.translation: What ! to Antipho ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.753c
sections.translation: The very same , I say .

sections.label: Section 5.1.754
sections.translation: What ? Has he got two wives ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.754b
sections.original: Au , obsecro : unam ille quidem hanc solam .
sections.translation: Dear no , prithee , he has only got this one .

sections.label: Section 5.1.755
sections.original: Quid illam alteram quae dicitur cognata ?
sections.translation: What about the other one that ’ s called his relative ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.755b
sections.translation: Why , this is she .

sections.label: Section 5.1.755c
sections.translation: What is it you say ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.756
sections.original: Composito factum est quomodo hanc amans habere posset
sections.translation: It was done on purpose , in order that her lover might be enabled to marry her without a portion .

sections.label: Section 5.1.757b
sections.original: Di vestram fidem , quam saepe forte temere
sections.translation: Ye Gods , by our trust in you ! How often do those things come about through accident , which you couldn ’ t dare to hope for ? On my return , I have found my daughter matched with the very person I wished , and just as I wanted ;

sections.label: Section 5.1.760
sections.original: Quod nos ambo opere maximo dabamus operam ut fieret ,
sections.translation: a thing that we were both using our endeavors , with the greatest earnestness , to bring about . Without any very great management on our part , by her own management , she has by herself brought this about .

sections.label: Section 5.1.762
sections.original: Nunc quid opus facto sit vide . Pater adolescentis venit ;
sections.translation: Now consider what ’ s to be done . The young man ’ s father has returned , and they say that he bears this with feelings highly offended .

sections.label: Section 5.1.763b
sections.translation: There ’ s no danger of that . But , by Gods and men , do take care that no one comes to know that she ’ s my daughter .

sections.label: Section 5.1.765
sections.translation: No one shall know it from me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.765b
sections.translation: Follow me ; in-doors we ’ ll hear the rest . ( He goes into DEMIPHO ’ S house , followed by SOPHRONA .)

sections.label: Section 5.2.766
sections.original: Nostrapte culpa facimus ut malis expediat esse ,
sections.translation: ’ Tis caused by our own fault , that it is advantageous to be dishonest ; while we wish ourselves to be styled very honest and generous . So run away as not to run beyond the house , [*] as the saying is . Was it not enough to receive an injury from him , but money must be voluntarily offered him as well ,
